name: opensearch-py (OpenSearch Python Client)

auth.notes: Basic auth (user/pass), API key, or AWS SigV4 for OpenSearch Serverless

performance.notes: Network-bound; bulk operations significantly improve throughput

agent_readiness.notes: OpenSearch Python client — fork of elasticsearch-py. from opensearchpy import OpenSearch. client = OpenSearch(hosts=[{'host': 'localhost', 'port': 9200}], http_auth=('admin', 'admin'), use_ssl=True). client.index(index='my-index', body={'title': 'test', 'content': 'hello'}). client.search(index='my-index', body={'query': {'match': {'content': 'hello'}}}). client.indices.create(index='my-idx', body=mapping). Bulk: from opensearchpy.helpers import bulk; bulk(client, actions). Async: AsyncOpenSearch. AWS: AWSV4SignerAuth + RequestsAWSV4SignerAuth. opensearch-py API mirrors elasticsearch-py — easy migration. Scroll API for large result sets.

gotchas: Forked from elasticsearch-py — API is nearly identical but some versions lag
AWS OpenSearch Service vs self-hosted OpenSearch have different auth patterns
SSL/TLS certificate verification important for production
Scroll API deprecated in newer versions — use point-in-time (PIT) instead
AWS SigV4 requires additional aws4auth or requests-aws4auth package
best_when: OpenSearch self-hosted or AWS OpenSearch Service clusters
Full-text search and analytics on AWS infrastructure
avoid_when: Elasticsearch clusters (use elasticsearch-py for version compatibility)
Simple keyword search (consider typesense or meilisearch)
summary: opensearch-py is the official OpenSearch Python client (fork of elasticsearch-py). from opensearchpy import OpenSearch; client = OpenSearch(hosts=[...], http_auth=('admin','pass')). client.index(index='idx', body={...}); client.search(index='idx', body={'query': {...}}). Bulk: helpers.bulk(client, actions). Async: AsyncOpenSearch. Apache-2.0 by AWS. Nearly identical to elasticsearch-py. Best for OpenSearch/AWS OpenSearch Service clusters.
